language.label: Language
language.hint: Language used for the Acorn interface. New translations are added gradually.

fontFamily.label: Font family
fontFamily.hint: Comma-separated stack. First family that resolves wins.

fontWeight.label: Font weight
fontWeight.hint: Weight for normal text. Effective values depend on the chosen font's available weights.

boldFontWeight.label: Bold font weight
boldFontWeight.hint: Weight used when the terminal renders bold cells.

lineHeight.label: Line height
lineHeight.hint: Cell-height multiplier. 1.00 packs rows flush; raise for more vertical breathing room. Range 1.00-2.00.

openLinksOn.label: Open links on
openLinksOn.hint: How to follow URLs that xterm detects in terminal output.

click.label: Click (default)
click.description: A single mouse click opens the URL in your default browser.

modifierClick.description: Hold {modifier} while clicking to open. Stops stray clicks on output containing URLs from yanking focus to the browser.

confirmRemove.label: Confirm before removing a session
confirmRemove.hint: Isolated worktrees always prompt because the delete-worktree choice still matters.

closeOnExit.label: Close tab when the process exits
closeOnExit.hint: When the session's shell or agent exits (e.g. you type `exit`), close the tab automatically instead of showing the press-Enter restart prompt. The worktree is preserved either way.

background.title: Background sessions
background.description: The acornd daemon owns long-running PTYs so terminal sessions survive Acorn restarts.

controlCli.label: Control sessions (acorn-ipc CLI)
controlCli.hint: Control sessions can dispatch commands to siblings via the acorn-ipc CLI. The CLI ships next to the app; symlink it into your $PATH to use it from any terminal.

refreshInterval.label: Refresh interval
refreshInterval.hint: How often the PRs and Actions tabs auto-refetch from gh. Lower values feel snappier but spend more API budget.

github.manualRefresh: Manual refresh (the icon in each tab) always works regardless of this interval.

listDensity.label: List density
listDensity.hint: Author avatars make rows easier to scan at a glance, but each row gets thicker.

showAuthorAvatars.label: Show author avatars
showAuthorAvatars.description: Render the GitHub avatar next to each PR row.

uiScale.label: UI scale
uiScale.hint: Scales the app chrome in 5% steps. Terminal text keeps its separate size setting.

theme.label: Theme
theme.hint: Built-in palettes and valid CSS files from the themes folder.

background.label: Background image
background.hint: One image can be applied to the app area, terminal area, or both.

title.label: Session title
title.hint: Which field becomes the bold first line of each sidebar session row.

name.label: Session name
name.description: The editable name you give each session (default).

metadata.label: Additional metadata
metadata.hint: Secondary line under the title. Toggle off everything for a single-line row.

status.label: Status
status.description: Idle / Running / Needs input / Failed / Completed.

icons.label: Inline icons
icons.hint: Glyphs that decorate each session row. Hide to make the list denser.

statusDot.label: Status dot
statusDot.description: Colored bullet at the row start. Redundant when Status is enabled in metadata.

sessionKind.label: Session kind icons
sessionKind.description: Branch glyph for isolated worktrees and bot glyph for control sessions.

hover.label: Show details on hover
hover.hint: Pop a tooltip with every field when hovering a session row, regardless of which fields the row itself shows.

statusBar.label: Status bar
statusBar.hint: Choose which optional badges show in the bottom status bar.

sessionCount.label: Session count
sessionCount.description: Total number of sessions across the active project (`sessions: N`).

activeSessionStatus.label: Active session status
activeSessionStatus.description: Lifecycle state of the active session (Idle / Running / Needs input / Failed / Completed).

githubAccount.label: GitHub account
githubAccount.description: The `gh` account used to list pull requests for the active repo.

workingDirectory.label: Working directory
workingDirectory.description: The active session's worktree path (tildified against $HOME).

memoryUsage.label: Memory usage
memoryUsage.description: Live memory readout for acorn and its child shells. Disabling also stops the 2-second polling loop, so it costs nothing when hidden.

command.label: Editor command
command.hint: Leave blank for the OS default. Examples: "code", "cursor --wait", "subl", "idea". The file path is appended as the last argument.
command.description: Used by the "Open in editor" right-click action on diff and staged file lists.

system.label: System notifications
system.hint: Send a macOS notification when a session changes status.

needsInput.label: Needs input
needsInput.description: Claude is waiting on the user.

test.label: Test
test.hint: Verifies the OS permission and that a notification can actually appear, independent of the Enable / Trigger settings above.

result.sent: Sent. Check your notification center if it didn't appear.
result.denied: Permission denied. Allow Acorn under System Settings -> Notifications.
result.failed: Failed to send. See the console for details.

notifications.permissionHint: macOS may ask once for permission the first time a notification fires.

intro.before: The selected agent powers acorn's AI features - currently the merge dialog's

agent.label: Agent
agent.hint: Choose which AI CLI acorn uses.

customOption.label: Custom command
customOption.description: Use any CLI not in the list. Whitespace-separated; no shell expansion.

ollamaModel.label: Ollama model
ollamaModel.hint: Passed to `ollama run <model>`. Defaults to `llama3` when blank.

llmModel.label: llm model
llmModel.hint: Passed via `llm -m <model>` (and `llm chat -m <model>` for sessions). Blank uses the llm-configured default.

customCommand.label: Custom command
customCommand.hint: Used for both interactive sessions and one-shot AI generation. Falls back to Claude Code when blank.

agents.requirement: The selected agent's CLI must already be installed and authenticated on this machine. Surfaces a clear error when the binary is missing.

storage.title: Reclaimable cache
storage.description: Disk artifacts that acorn cannot clean up through its normal session lifecycle (e.g. files orphaned by a crash or by edits made while acorn was offline). Sessions, projects, settings, and live terminal scrollback are never touched.

scrollbackOrphans.label: Orphan terminal scrollback
scrollbackOrphans.description: ANSI scrollback files left behind by sessions that no longer exist (e.g. removed while acorn was offline, or before the prune-on-boot logic existed). Live sessions' scrollback is not touched - only the unreclaimable leftovers are surfaced.

confirm.message: This will permanently delete the cached data listed above ({size}). Continue?

experiments.warning: Unfinished features. Behavior may change between releases; the toggles themselves are stable.

stickyPrompt.label: Pin last user prompt above terminal
stickyPrompt.description: Detects the most recent claude prompt line in the rendered terminal buffer and pins it at the top of the pane so it stays in view while reading the reply. Cmd+K clears it along with the rest of the scrollback.

cjkCellWidthHeuristic.label: CJK terminal cell-width correction
cjkCellWidthHeuristic.description: Heuristic for CJK monospaced fonts (D2Coding, Sarasa Mono, etc.): when `W` and `ga` measure the same width, halves the cell width so CJK glyphs sit on the cell grid. Skipped when widths differ, so ASCII fonts with system CJK fallback are not corrected. Known limitation: tab switches can briefly show a 1-frame glyph-size flicker as xterm's resize path resets `cell.width` to the natural W advance before the addon reapplies the halved value. Off by default.

resumeModal.label: Show "Resume previous conversation" modal at cold boot
resumeModal.description: On Acorn launch, probes every persisted session for an unfinished claude or codex transcript and pops a one-shot modal when you focus the session so you can pick up where you left off. Disable to suppress the modal entirely.

about.title: About Acorn
about.description: Acorn checks for updates automatically on startup and once every 24 hours. You can also check manually below.

aria.newControlSessionInProject: New control session in this project

empty.dropTabOrDoubleClick: Drop a tab here or double-click to start a session
empty.createProject: Create a project to get started. Double-click here.

toasts.shellEnvReloaded: Shell environment reloaded. Open a new session to apply.

daemon.label: Daemon
daemon.hint: The acornd daemon owns long-running terminal sessions. With the daemon on, Acorn can quit and reopen without losing your PTYs. Off falls back to the legacy in-process path - sessions die with the app.
daemon.enabledDescription: Sessions persist across Acorn restarts until you explicitly quit them.
daemon.disabledDescription: Sessions are bound to this Acorn process - closing the app kills them.

status.label: Status
status.hint: Live state of the running daemon. Updates every 3 seconds while this panel is open.

controls.label: Controls
controls.hint: Restart reconnects the bridge - useful after killing the daemon from a terminal. Quit kills every running PTY and exits the daemon.

sessions.label: Sessions
sessions.hint: Every PTY the daemon currently tracks. Dead rows can be forgotten from the daemon side; their app metadata stays so you can resume from disk if the agent supports it.
sessions.disabled: Daemon disabled - sessions are managed by the legacy in-process path.

actions.restoreTooltip: Adopt this session back into Acorn's sidebar
actions.forgetTooltip: Remove this row from the daemon registry

metadata.orphaned: No app-side row - orphaned in daemon.
